name: Outstanding Chocolate Cake
recipeIngredient: 2 ounces unsweetened chocolate, melted and cooled
recipeInstructions:
This is a 45-year-old recipe from Illinois that our Farm Journal tasting staff rated "fantastic." Tender crumbed, deep chocolate flavor, with an ultra-creamy frosting that tastes like chocolate whipped cream.
Whipped chocolate frosting (recipe follows):
Sift together cake flour, baking powder, and salt; set aside. Cream butter and sugar in a mixing bowl until light and fluffy, using an electric mixer at medium speed. Add eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition. Blend in vanilla and cooled chocolate, mixing well. Combine baking soda and cold water; stir until baking soda is dissolved. Add dry ingredients alternately with the cold water mixture to the creamed mixture, beating well after each addition. Pour batter into a greased 13 x 9 x 2 inch baking pan. Bake in a 350°F oven for 35 minutes or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean. Cool in the pan on a rack for 10 minutes. Remove from the pan and cool on a rack. Frost sides and top of cake with whipped chocolate frosting. Cut into squares. Makes 16 servings.
Whipped Chocolate Frosting:
Combine 2 (1 oz) squares unsweetened chocolate and 2 tablespoons butter or regular margarine in a small saucepan. Heat over low heat until chocolate and butter are melted. Cool slightly. Combine cooled chocolate mixture, 1 cup sifted confectioners sugar, 1 egg, 1/8 teaspoon salt, 1/4 cup milk, and 1 teaspoon vanilla in a metal mixing bowl. Set bowl in another bowl filled with ice cubes and water. Beat with an electric mixer at high speed until the mixture becomes light and fluffy and forms soft peaks, about 5 minutes.
